steps.text: Find the outer and inner radii. Outside diameter = 60 in $\Rightarrow$ outer radius $R = 30$ in. Wall thickness = $\frac{5}{8}$ in $\Rightarrow$ inner radius $r = 30 - \frac{5}{8} = \frac{235}{8}$ in.
Compute $R^2 - r^2$ using the difference-of-squares identity: $(R-r)(R+r) = \frac{5}{8} \times \left(30 + \frac{235}{8}\right) = \frac{5}{8} \times \frac{475}{8} = \frac{2375}{64}$.
Apply the hollow cylinder volume formula: $V = \pi h(R^2 - r^2) = \pi \times 132 \times \frac{2375}{64} = \frac{313500\pi}{64} \approx 15{,}391$ cubic inches.

Evaluate all choices:
  • A: 162 — far too small; this is roughly $\pi \times 5/8 \times 1$, not accounting for the full height or circumference. Eliminated.
  • B: 518 — still far too small for a 132-inch-tall pipe with a 60-inch diameter. Eliminated.
  • C: 2,475 — about 6 times too small; possibly from omitting $\pi$ or using wrong dimensions. Eliminated.
  • D: 15,389 — matches $\frac{313500\pi}{64} \approx 15{,}391$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
